description: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 received assent of the President on 11 August 2023.
Act shall come into force on such date as Central Government may appoint; different dates for different provisions.
Notification G.S.R. 846(E) dated 13th November, 2025 published the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.
Rules 1 (short title and commencement), 2 (definitions), and 17 to 21 (appointment, salary, meetings, digital office, terms of officers) came into force on the date of publication.

event: Rule 4 (Consent Manager registration) comes into force
description: Rule 4 shall come into force one year after the date of publication of the Rules (i.e., 13 November 2026).
Rules 3, 5 to 16, 22 and 23 shall come into force eighteen months after publication, i.e., 13 May 2027 (approximately).
Appeal must be filed within 60 days from date of receipt of order or direction of the Board.
Inquiry by Board shall be completed within 6 months from receipt of intimation/complaint/reference/direction, extendable by up to 3 months at a time.
Appellate Tribunal shall endeavor to dispose of appeal within 6 months from date of presentation.
Central Government may amend Schedule but cannot increase penalty to more than twice the original amount.
Central Government cannot make orders under section 43 after expiry of 3 years from Act commencement.
